assertion.title: Fused variable-repeat operators reduce total nodes 196→192
assertion.statement: Adding 3 operators that fuse compound expressions where variables repeat saves 4 nodes across 3 equations, reducing total nodes from 196 to 192 while maintaining 50/50 exact matches.

details.hypothesis: Equations where the same variable appears multiple times in the expression tree (eq13 c/(c-v), eq34 gaussian(x/sigma), eq40 x³/(exp(x)-1)) can be fused into single operators that eliminate redundant variable references, saving 1-2 nodes each.
changes: Updated permutation templates to use compact forms directly
details.key_insight: When a variable appears multiple times in a compound expression (e.g., x/(x-y) uses x twice, x³/(exp(x)-1) uses x in both numerator and denominator), fusing into a single operator eliminates the repeated variable node. The planck(x) unary op is the most efficient, collapsing 4 nodes (div+cube+expm1+var+var) into just 2 (planck+var). The remaining equations at 3+ nodes use distinct variables or have irreducible structure.
